- **CU-002:** Appears to be responding well with improving weight trajectory.

### PK model stability note
Based on the dosing configuration alone, the parameters appear internally consistent and there are no obvious anomalies in the listed compassionate-use entries. However, this file does **not** include live model health, validation outputs, or monitoring status, so it is **not sufficient by itself** to confirm real-time PK model stability.

### Caution

**Compassionate-use dosing summary**
- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)

|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| **CU-001** | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable; 22% tumor regression at last scan |
| **CU-002** | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| **CU-003** | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment; first dose given 2026-03-27 |

On your second question: **nothing in this dosing file suggests an obvious PK issue** — the entries look internally consistent, and the patient notes read as clinically stable/positive overall. That said, **this file alone doesn’t show live PK model health or monitoring status**, so I can’t fully confirm real-time model stability just from the config.

I also saved this as a report for Dr. Martinez. If you want, I can reformat it into a shorter email-ready version.
